title: Create assistant observability incident from telemetry
given: A demo operator has settings or operations view/edit access.
The tenant has assistant audit events, action queue items, release gates, automation policies, and shadow runs.
Some answers have weak evidence coverage or negative feedback.
then: The assistant persists an AssistantObservabilityIncident row.
The incident includes health snapshot, failure signals, drift signals, evidence gaps, automation risks, degraded mode, rollback plan, and postmortem.
No automation policy, release gate, prompt, audit event, or source business record is changed automatically.

title: Queue incident response before rollback or degraded mode changes
given: An observability incident exists with high severity or automation risk.
The operator has settings or operations edit access.
when: The operator adds a review note.
then: An AssistantActionQueueItem is created for assistant observability incident review.
The incident status changes to REVIEW_QUEUED.
The audit event states automation policies, release gates, prompts, and source records were not changed automatically.

title: Resolve incident after human postmortem review
given: An incident has a postmortem summary, root-cause hypotheses, and rollback steps.
The operator has reviewed evidence coverage and automation risk.
when: The operator enters a resolution note.
then: The incident status changes to RESOLVED with resolver and timestamp metadata.
A resolution audit event is recorded.
Resolution does not imply automation was re-enabled or release gates were passed without separate approval.
